title: CHECKSUMS drift — tampered migration is detected
steps:
You are in the project root with .venv active (see 'Before you start').

This scenario modifies a file briefly and then restores it. Do NOT commit between steps.

1. Save an untouched copy of the migration:
   cp src/orchestrator/db/migrations/0001_initial.sql /tmp/saved-0001.sql

2. Tamper with the file by appending a comment:
   echo '-- tampered for UAT' >> src/orchestrator/db/migrations/0001_initial.sql

3. Remove any stale test DB:
   rm -f /tmp/orch-uat-drift.db

4. Try to run migrations. It should fail:
   python -c "from orchestrator.db.migrate import run_migrations; run_migrations('/tmp/orch-uat-drift.db')"; echo "exit=$?"

5. Restore the original file immediately:
   cp /tmp/saved-0001.sql src/orchestrator/db/migrations/0001_initial.sql

6. Verify the file is restored (no diff against git):
   git diff --exit-code src/orchestrator/db/migrations/0001_initial.sql && echo RESTORED
expected:
Step 4: exits with a non-zero 'exit=' line (expect exit=1). Traceback ends in a MigrationError whose message contains 'checksum mismatch' and the text 'file=' and 'manifest='.

Step 6: prints 'RESTORED' on its own line. git diff --exit-code returns 0.

PASS if step 4 raised MigrationError AND step 6 printed RESTORED. FAIL if step 4 succeeded silently (bug — the CHECKSUMS check didn't fire) or step 6 shows a diff (your file needs manual restore: git checkout -- src/orchestrator/db/migrations/0001_initial.sql).
